title: ranunculus cantoniensis subsp. tachiroi
intro: Ranunculus cantoniensis subsp. tachiroi is a plant species in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae), also known as Tachiro's ranunculus, which is native to Taiwan and Japan. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Ranunculus cantoniensis subsp. tachiroi include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is used in herbal preparations, often in the form of a decoction or infusion, to promote overall well-being and improve immune function.
